channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Queen Grace Mugabe Bounces Back: The Secret Thug Army and the Brutal AK-47 Assault on Mazowe Miners
item.description: Mazowe – The tranquil hills of Mazowe, usually echoing with the rhythmic sounds of artisanal gold mining, were this week shattered by a scene of unbridled brutality. Former First Lady Grace Mugabe stands accused of orchestrating a violent, military-style raid on artisanal gold miners, an incident that has cast a long shadow over the region [...] The post Queen Grace Mugabe Bounces Back: The Secret Thug Army and the Brutal AK-47 Assault on Mazowe Miners first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe-Eligible Camron Mpofu Signs Manchester United Academy Scholarship @ManUtd
item.description: Zimbabwe-Eligible Camron Mpofu Signs Manchester United Academy Scholarship Zimbabwe-eligible youngster Camron Mpofu has moved a step closer to achieving his dream of playing for Manchester United’s senior team after being awarded a first-year academy scholarship by the English club. The 16-year-old forward has been included in the club’s latest intake of academy scholars for the [...] The post Zimbabwe-Eligible Camron Mpofu Signs Manchester United Academy Scholarship appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Masasi High Students Walk Free After Being Sentenced To 10 Years For Rape
item.description: Masasi Students Sentenced To 10 Years, File Appeal At High Court Two former Masasi High School students sentenced to 10 years in prison after being convicted of raping a schoolmate have approached the High Court. The pair are challenging both their conviction and sentence following a case that drew widespread attention. The two were aged [...] The post Masasi High Students Walk Free After Being Sentenced To 10 Years For Rape appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Lebo M claims Zimbabweans want to kill him @bulawayo24
item.description: Renowned South African composer Lebo M has sparked widespread debate after claiming that Zimbabweans are trying to kill him over his US$27 million lawsuit against United States-based Zimbabwean comedi... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Biti’s group launches campaign to overturn Mnangagwa’s constitutional changes @bulawayo24
item.description: A broad coalition of churches, labour unions, civic organisations, students, war veterans and opposition political figures has launched a nationwide campaign to challenge Zimbabwe’s newly enacted cons... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.description: Dynamos claimed a 2-1 victory over Hardrock FC after the final 14 minutes of their abandoned Castle Lager Premier Soccer League fixture were played behind closed doors at Baobab Stadium on Thursday.Th... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Zimbabwean goalkeeper signs new Aston Villa deal
item.description: Young Zimbabwean goalkeeper earns a fresh Aston Villa contract as the highly rated talent continues his impressive rise in English football. The post Zimbabwean goalkeeper signs new Aston Villa deal appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: ’Mnangagwa made a constitutional blunder.’ says Mwonzora @bulawayo24
item.description: The Movement for Democratic Change (MDC) has approached the Constitutional Court seeking to have Constitution Amendment Act No. 3 declared unconstitutional, arguing that President Emmerson Mnangagwa a... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Malema’s EFF blasts Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Amendment Act No. 3 @bulawayo24 @EFFSouthAfrica @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: South Africa’s opposition party, the Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF), has condemned Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Amendment Act No. 3, accusing the ruling ZANU PF of placing its grip on power above the in... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: ZimStat, National Competitiveness Commission embark on nationwide survey to assess local authorities
item.description: THE Zimbabwe National Statistics Agency (ZimStat), in collaboration with the National Competitiveness Commission (NCC), has begun a nationwide survey that will assess and rank the competitiveness of all 92 local authorities as part of efforts to strengthen governance, improve service delivery and attract investment. The 2026 Rural and Urban Councils Competitiveness Index (RUCCI) Survey, which [...] The post ZimStat, National Competitiveness Commission embark on nationwide survey to assess local authorities appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe Police Monitoring Ports of Entry as UK Hunts Zimbabwean Wanted Over Wife, Daughters’ Deaths @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: Zimbabwe Police Monitoring Ports of Entry as UK Hunts Zimbabwean Wanted Over Wife, Daughters’ Deaths The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) says it is closely monitoring the country’s borders as British authorities continue searching for a Zimbabwean-born man wanted in connection with the deaths of his wife and two daughters in the United Kingdom. Police say [...] The post Zimbabwe Police Monitoring Ports of Entry as UK Hunts Zimbabwean Wanted Over Wife, Daughters’ Deaths appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Zimbabwe police yet to receive official communication from UK over Bedford triple murder suspect Tshuma @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: BBC The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) say they have not been officially contacted by UK authorities about triple murder suspect Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma. The 45-year-old is wanted over the deaths of his wife Nothabo Zandile Tshuma, 42, and daughters Natalie, 15, and Nala, five, who were found dead at the family home near Bedford on [...] The post Zimbabwe police yet to receive official communication from UK over Bedford triple murder suspect Tshuma appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Dynamos edge Hardrock FC in resumed PSL match, claims 2-1 victory
item.description: Dynamos secured a 2-1 victory over Hardrock FC after the remaining 14 minutes of their abandoned Castle Lager Premier Soccer League fixture were played behind closed doors at Baobab Stadium on Thursday. The match resumed from the 76th minute with the teams locked at 1-1 after the original fixture, played at Chahwanda Stadium in May, [...] The post Dynamos edge Hardrock FC in resumed PSL match, claims 2-1 victory appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Mwonzora takes Mnangagwa’s term extension to ConCourt, says President made a constitutional blunder
item.description: The Movement for Democratic Change (MDC) has approached the Constitutional Court seeking to overturn the Constitution Amendment Act No. 3, arguing that President Emmerson Mnangagwa acted unlawfully by signing the legislation into law without first submitting it to a national referendum. The application, filed by MDC leader Douglas Mwonzora, challenges the constitutionality of the recently [...] The post Mwonzora takes Mnangagwa’s term extension to ConCourt, says President made a constitutional blunder appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwean families driven from their homes camp outside Limpopo police station
item.description: GroundUp LIMPOPO – Chivhayo Sinorema says he has lived in Khuvhi village, Thohoyandou, since 2009, surviving on odd jobs in construction sector, cutting wood or gardening, and sending money to support his wife and two children in Zimbabwe. Then a week ago, a mob accompanied by his landlord, told him he must leave. We spoke [...] The post Zimbabwean families driven from their homes camp outside Limpopo police station appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News
